title: carex canescens subsp. disjuncta
intro: Carex canescens subsp. disjuncta is a subspecies of sedge, a type of perennial grass-like plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to North America and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Carex canescens subsp. disjuncta can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
